title: Lesson 1: (Kana) - Lesson 1: (Hiragana) "あ, い, う, え, お"

intro_cards.title: Welcome to Hiragana
intro_cards.body:
Hiragana is one of three Japanese writing systems. It's a syllabary — unlike an alphabet where each letter is a single sound (like 'b' or 'a'), each hiragana character represents a whole syllable (like 'ka' or 'shi').

Japanese has no spaces between words. You'll learn to recognize word boundaries through practice.

There are 46 basic hiragana characters. Let's start with the five vowels: あ, い, う, え, お.
